expectations.text: Identifies user as higher rate taxpayer (40%)
expectations.evidence: Lines 61-64 confirm higher rate status: 'Higher rate band: £50,271 onwards — your non-savings income of £58,030 places you £7,760 into the higher rate band' and line 74 explicitly states 'higher rate taxpayer'

expectations.text: Applies dividend allowance (£500) and taxes excess at 33.75%
expectations.evidence: Lines 90-102 state: 'Dividend allowance: £500, Taxable dividends: £2,900, Tax on dividends: £2,900 × 33.75% = £978.75'

expectations.text: Applies PSA correctly (£500 for higher rate) to savings interest
expectations.evidence: Lines 72-86 state: 'Personal Savings Allowance (PSA) is £500 (not the £1,000 available to basic rate taxpayers)' and 'Taxable savings interest: £450, Tax on savings interest: £450 × 40% = £180'

expectations.text: Explains SIP free shares held 3 years: partial income tax applies on sale
expectations.evidence: Lines 25-37 explain: 'Shares held 3 to 5 years: the lower of the market value at award or market value at withdrawal is taxed as employment income + NI. This is partial relief only — there is still a tax and NI charge.'

expectations.text: Explains SIP partnership shares bought from pre-tax salary
expectations.evidence: Lines 39-43 state: 'You purchased these from gross salary — meaning you did not pay Income Tax or NI on the £1,800 used to buy them. This benefit has already been received.'

expectations.text: Applies CGT annual exempt amount (£3,000) to the £2,800 gain — no CGT due
expectations.evidence: Lines 109-117 state: 'Your gain of £2,800 is below the £3,000 exempt amount — no CGT is due.'

expectations.text: Confirms ISA income/gains are completely tax-free and not reportable
expectations.evidence: Lines 104-105 state: 'ISA dividends are completely tax-free and not reported. Your £10,000 ISA contribution is simply sheltering your investments — any dividends or growth within that wrapper are irrelevant for your tax return.'

expectations.text: Recommends bed and ISA strategy to shelter GIA holdings
expectations.evidence: Lines 161-171 explain bed and ISA strategy: 'Sell the shares in your GIA, Check the gain against your £3,000 annual exempt amount, Immediately repurchase the same shares inside your Stocks and Shares ISA' with note that '30-day anti-avoidance rule does not apply when you rebuy inside an ISA'

expectations.text: Provides numerical tax calculations with workings
expectations.evidence: Lines 121-137 provide detailed 'Full Tax Bill Summary' with tax calculation table showing all components (employment + SIP, savings, dividends, CGT) with rates and tax amounts clearly shown for each
